sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The game restarted. I clicked [55, 14] and it turned into a magenta square. The timer reset to full.
Now I need to continue clicking the sky blue dots in path order. The next dot in my hypothesized path is [41, 16].
But wait, I also have [57, 16] at column 16. Which one should I click?
Let me look at the image. The magenta square is at [55, 14]-[56, 15] (2x2). The remaining sky blue dots are at:
- [37, 18], [37, 41], [40, 37], [41, 16], [54, 49], [56, 47], [57, 16]
Let me continue with the path: [41, 16] next.
Actually, let me reconsider. In Level 1, the path was a diagonal from bottom-left to top-right. The gray dots formed a clear diagonal path.
In Level 2, the sky blue dots might also form a diagonal path. Let me look at the positions:
- [55, 14] - bottom-left
- [41, 16] - middle-left
- [37, 18] - top-left
- [40, 37] - middle
- [37, 41] - top-right
- [56, 47] - bottom-right
- [54, 49] - bottom-right
- [57, 16] - bottom-left
The dots at [55, 14] and [57, 16] are close to each other (bottom-left). The dots at [41, 16] and [37, 18] are close to each other (middle-left). The dots at [40, 37] and [37, 41] are close to each other (middle-right). The dots at [56, 47] and [54, 49] are close to each other (bottom-right).
So there are 4 pairs of dots, and I need to choose one from each pair to form the path.
Looking at the pairs:
- Pair 1: [55, 14] and [57, 16] - I clicked [55, 14]
- Pair 2: [41, 16] and [37, 18] - need to choose one
- Pair 3: [40, 37] and [37, 41] - need to choose one
- Pair 4: [56, 47] and [54, 49] - need to choose one
If the path is a diagonal from bottom-left to top-right, the rows should decrease as columns increase. Let me check:
- [55, 14] → row 55, col 14
- Next: row should be less than 55, col should be greater than 14
  - [41, 16]: row 41 < 55, col 16 > 14 ✓
  - [37, 18]: row 37 < 55, col 18 > 14 ✓
  - [57, 16]: row 57 > 55, col 16 > 14 ✗ (row increased)
So [57, 16] is not on the diagonal path. I should choose between [41, 16] and [37, 18].
- [41, 16]: row 41, col 16
- [37, 18]: row 37, col 18
Both are valid next steps. Let me continue with [41, 16] (closer to [55, 14]).
Actually, wait. The pairs might not be what I think. Let me reconsider.
Looking at the positions again:
- [37, 18], [37, 41] - same row 37
- [40, 37] - row 40
- [41, 16] - row 41
- [54, 49] - row 54
- [55, 14] - row 55 (clicked)
- [56, 47] - row 56
- [57, 16] - row 57
The dots at rows 37 and 41 are close (4 rows apart). The dots at rows 54 and 57 are close (3 rows apart).
Actually, I think the pairs are:
- [37, 18] and [41, 16] - close to each other (rows 37 and 41, columns 18 and 16)
- [37, 41] and [40, 37] - close to each other (rows 37 and 40, columns 41 and 37)
- [54, 49] and [56, 47] - close to each other (rows 54 and 56, columns 49 and 47)
- [55, 14] and [57, 16] - close to each other (rows 55 and 57, columns 14 and 16)
I clicked [55, 14] from the last pair. The next pair is [37, 18] and [41, 16]. Let me click [41, 16] next.
